task_completion.description: Create the test page at /home/ga/web_audit/index.html with serviceWorker and localStorage checks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the creation of /home/ga/web_audit/index.html containing JavaScript that references 'navigator.serviceWorker' and 'localStorage' (specifically managing the 'audit_token' key).

task_completion.description: Host the page on port 8080 and log output to /home/ga/Documents/server.log.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command starting an HTTP server on port 8080 (e.g., python3 -m http.server 8080) with output redirected or piped to /home/ga/Documents/server.log.

task_completion.description: Open Tor Browser and navigate to the local server to observe initial API behavior.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tor Browser window open with 'localhost:8080' or '127.0.0.1:8080' in the address bar, displaying the test page.

task_completion.description: Document the initial API behavior in /home/ga/Documents/initial_audit.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the contents of /home/ga/Documents/initial_audit.txt being written or viewed, describing the initial state of serviceWorker and localStorage.

task_completion.description: Perform a complete browser restart and verify persistence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing Tor Browser being closed and subsequently reopened, then navigating back to the test page to check if the 'audit_token' persisted.
A text editor or terminal showing the contents of /home/ga/Documents/restart_audit.txt being written or viewed, describing whether the localStorage data persisted after the restart.

integrity.description: Verify that Tor Browser was used for the audit, not a standard browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser window should display Tor Browser specific UI elements, such as the Onion logo, Tor-specific toolbar icons, or 'Tor Browser' in the window title.

integrity.description: Verify that the browser was actually restarted, not just refreshed.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be a clear sequence showing the browser window being completely closed (e.g., returning to desktop/terminal) and a new instance being launched.

integrity.description: Verify that the audit files were written based on actual observation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The creation of initial_audit.txt and restart_audit.txt should occur chronologically after the respective interactions with Tor Browser, rather than being written beforehand.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The test page must be at /home/ga/web_audit/index.html. The local server must run on port 8080 and log to /home/ga/Documents/server.log. The initial audit must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/initial_audit.txt, and the post-restart audit to /home/ga/Documents/restart_audit.txt.
